input.headline: How to turn hazelnuts into a brilliant flour for cakes – recipe

raw.headline: How to turn hazelnuts into a brilliant flour for cakes – recipe

winner: Why did the baker go nuts at the supermarket? Because he found an aisle marked "Brilliant Hazelnut Flour"!

candidates.text: Why don't hazelnuts make good detectives? Because they always grind the case to a fine powder!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is clever with a play on words ('grind the case to a fine powder'), but it's slightly forced and not fully aligned with the recipe context in the headline."}

candidates.text: Why did the baker refuse to argue with his girlfriend while making hazelnut flour? Because he knew it would turn into a nutty debate!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, uses wordplay with 'nutty debate,' and ties into the topic of hazelnuts. However, it's somewhat generic and lacks deeper specificity related to flour-making."}

candidates.text: Now I can finally bake a cake so bright, you'll need sunglasses...just remember, the flour came from hazelnuts.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise and ties the headline to a humorous outcome, but the surprise and specificity could be stronger."}

candidates.text: Why did the baker go nuts at the supermarket? Because he found an aisle marked "Brilliant Hazelnut Flour"!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat related to the headline but lacks strong surprise or specificity; 'go nuts' is a common phrase and doesn't strongly tie to the flour recipe context."}

candidates.text: Why did the baker refuse to eat his own cake? Because it was made with "brilliant hazelnuts.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 4.5, "reason": "Lacks surprise and specificity; the pun on 'brilliant hazelnuts' is weak and not clearly connected to the headline."}
